tool_input.summary: Interpreting Arabia Terra fluvial depositional systems (e.g., channel/fan/delta-like deposits and inverted channels) typically places sustained river activity in the Late Noachian through Early Hesperian, i.e., around the Noachian–Hesperian transition (~3.9–3.6 Ga), with local uncertainty commonly a few ×10^8 years depending on crater-count statistics and stratigraphic relationships. The PDS datasets below are the core archives you’d use to map the deposits, build DTMs/topographic context, assess mineralogy, and (if desired) support crater-counting/stratigraphic age bracketing.

candidates.title: VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: CTX image base for regional mapping of fluvial depositional systems across Arabia Terra (planform morphology, unit contacts for stratigraphic bracketing).

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Global gridded MOLA topography (MEGDR) to quantify slopes, long profiles, catchments, and paleogradients that help interpret river systems and their stratigraphic setting.

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-5-IEGDR-L3-V2.0
candidates.reasoning: Improved/alternate MOLA global grid (IEGDR) often used for topographic context and map products; useful cross-check to MEGDR for geomorphic analyses.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format
candidates.reasoning: PDS4-form MOLA topography products (IMG) convenient for workflows that prefer PDS4 labels; supports the same topographic analyses used in river/fan interpretation and stratigraphic age bracketing.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format
candidates.reasoning: Quick-look and map-style MOLA topography images (plus IMG) for rapid regional context when correlating fluvial deposits to surrounding terrain units.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0
candidates.reasoning: HRSC regional imaging at intermediate resolution; useful for tying local fluvial deposits in Arabia Terra into broader stratigraphic/geomorphic context.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: HRSC-derived DTMs where available provide higher-resolution topography than global MOLA for channel/fan geometry, terrace levels, and paleoflow reconstructions relevant to timing interpretation.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MTRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: CRISM map-projected targeted reduced data (MTRDR) for mineralogical context (e.g., clays/sulfates) that can constrain aqueous episode timing relative to Noachian–Hesperian units.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-5-RDR-MPTARGETED-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 counterpart for CRISM map-projected targeted RDRs; included because many legacy workflows/papers use these products for stratigraphy/mineralogy of fluvial deposits.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD U.S. Radargrams Bundle
candidates.reasoning: SHARAD radargrams can help test subsurface architecture beneath depositional systems (e.g., buried channels/stratigraphy), which may help bracket formation/erosion timing.

candidates.title: MRO M SHARAD 3 EDR V1
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 SHARAD EDR archive version of radar observations, for users working in the legacy SHARAD pipeline or needing original-format radar data.

tool_calls.tool_output: Final result processed.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
What age range do these Arabia Terra fluvial depositional systems imply for river activity?

parts.args: {"summary":"Interpreting Arabia Terra fluvial depositional systems (e.g., channel/fan/delta-like deposits and inverted channels) typically places sustained river activity in the Late Noachian through Early Hesperian, i.e., around the Noachian–Hesperian transition (~3.9–3.6 Ga), with local uncertainty commonly a few ×10^8 years depending on crater-count statistics and stratigraphic relationships. The PDS datasets below are the core archives you’d use to map the deposits, build DTMs/topographic context, assess mineralogy, and (if desired) support crater-counting/stratigraphic age bracketing.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-CTX-2-EDR-L0-V1.0","path":null,"title":"VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"img","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"CTX image base for regional mapping of fluvial depositional systems across Arabia Terra (planform morphology, unit contacts for stratigraphic bracketing)."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0","path":null,"title":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Global gridded MOLA topography (MEGDR) to quantify slopes, long profiles, catchments, and paleogradients that help interpret river systems and their stratigraphic setting."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-IEGDR-L3-V2.0","path":null,"title":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-IEGDR-L3-V2.0","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Improved/alternate MOLA global grid (IEGDR) often used for topographic context and map products; useful cross-check to MEGDR for geomorphic analyses."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_data::1.0","path":null,"title":"MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"PDS4-form MOLA topography products (IMG) convenient for workflows that prefer PDS4 labels; supports the same topographic analyses used in river/fan interpretation and stratigraphic age bracketing."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_image::1.0","path":null,"title":"MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Quick-look and map-style MOLA topography images (plus IMG) for rapid regional context when correlating fluvial deposits to surrounding terrain units."},{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0","path":null,"title":"MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0","mission":"Mars Express","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"HRSC regional imaging at intermediate resolution; useful for tying local fluvial deposits in Arabia Terra into broader stratigraphic/geomorphic context."},{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0","path":null,"title":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0","mission":"Mars Express","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"HRSC-derived DTMs where available provide higher-resolution topography than global MOLA for channel/fan geometry, terrace levels, and paleoflow reconstructions relevant to timing interpretation."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_crism_mtrdr::1.0","path":null,"title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MTRDR Bundle","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"CRISM map-projected targeted reduced data (MTRDR) for mineralogical context (e.g., clays/sulfates) that can constrain aqueous episode timing relative to Noachian–Hesperian units."},{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-CRISM-5-RDR-MPTARGETED-V1.0","path":null,"title":"MRO-M-CRISM-5-RDR-MPTARGETED-V1.0","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"PDS3 counterpart for CRISM map-projected targeted RDRs; included because many legacy workflows/papers use these products for stratigraphy/mineralogy of fluvial deposits."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_sharad_us_radargrams::1.0","path":null,"title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter SHARAD U.S. Radargrams Bundle","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"SHARAD radargrams can help test subsurface architecture beneath depositional systems (e.g., buried channels/stratigraphy), which may help bracket formation/erosion timing."},{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-SHARAD-3-EDR-V1","path":null,"title":"MRO M SHARAD 3 EDR V1","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"PDS3 SHARAD EDR archive version of radar observations, for users working in the legacy SHARAD pipeline or needing original-format radar data."}]}
